title: AML Enhanced Due Diligence Protocol
regulatory_ref: BSA/AML, FINRA Rule 3310, FinCEN CDD Rule
content:
MEMORANDUM

TO: All Wealth Management Advisors and Senior Staff
FROM: Compliance Department
DATE: March 15, 2025
RE: Enhanced Due Diligence Protocol Implementation

Effective immediately, Meridian Wealth Partners is implementing enhanced Anti-Money Laundering (AML) procedures in accordance with the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA), FINRA Rule 3310, and the FinCEN Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Rule. This protocol applies to all client relationships exceeding $1.5 million in assets under management or exhibiting heightened risk characteristics.

TRIGGER EVENTS FOR ENHANCED DUE DILIGENCE:

1. Initial cash deposits or wire transfers exceeding $250,000 within a 30-day period
2. Client domicile or business operations in high-risk jurisdictions as defined by FATF
3. Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) or immediate family members
4. Complex ownership structures involving multiple legal entities
5. Unusual transaction patterns inconsistent with stated investment objectives

REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION AND VERIFICATION:

All advisors must obtain and verify the following within 15 business days of trigger event identification:

• Source of wealth documentation including tax returns, business financial statements, or inheritance documentation
• Enhanced identity verification using independent sources beyond standard KYC requirements
• Beneficial ownership information for entities controlling 25% or greater ownership interest
• Purpose and intended nature of the business relationship documentation
• Risk assessment questionnaire completion with annual updates mandatory

For example, when client Robert J. Williams recently transferred $850,000 from an offshore account, our team successfully implemented this protocol by securing certified tax returns, bank statements from the originating institution, and third-party verification of his consulting business revenues. The enhanced review was completed within the required timeframe, and Account #4821-7734 was approved for standard servicing.

MONITORING AND ONGOING OBLIGATIONS:

Continuous monitoring systems will flag transactions exceeding 125% of established baseline activity patterns. Advisors must document any client communications regarding unusual activity and maintain detailed records in our compliance management system. Monthly reviews are required for all enhanced due diligence accounts.

Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR) obligations remain unchanged. Any transactions reasonably suspected of money laundering must be reported within 30 days using FinCEN Form 109. Contact the compliance hotline at (212) 555-0147 for immediate consultation on potential SAR filings.

RECORDKEEPING REQUIREMENTS:

All enhanced due diligence files must be maintained for a minimum of five years following account closure. Digital records should be stored in the secure compliance database with appropriate access controls. Physical documents require locked storage in designated compliance areas.

The firm's AML Compliance Officer will conduct quarterly reviews of all enhanced due diligence cases to ensure adherence to these protocols and identify any systemic issues requiring attention. Training sessions will be scheduled monthly to address specific scenarios and regulatory updates.

Non-compliance with these enhanced procedures may result in regulatory sanctions, monetary penalties, and potential criminal liability for both individual advisors and the firm. Questions regarding specific client situations should be directed immediately to the Compliance Department before proceeding with any account activities.

This protocol supersedes all previous AML guidance and becomes effective for all new and existing client relationships meeting the specified criteria.
